MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Casas, Esq. (619-341-2110) of The Casas Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Arthur Chapman, Esq. (310-207-7722) of Chapman Glucksman & Dean for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Th Court shares his concerns re: the motion, deposition testimony, misappropriate images by independent contractor. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as agreed to by parties as follows:

DEFENDANTS HERITAGE MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC., MYRON WALTERS, CRAIG DULING and DAVID SOBEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED in its entirety. Defendants correctly cite Millsap v. Federal Express Corp. (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 425, 430 for the proposition that a hirer is generally not liable for the torts of the hired independent contractor. Defendants further show that J Dog was an independent contractor and defendants had no role in the postings. (Walters Dec., pars. 5-7; Sovey Dec., par. 4.) Plaintiff Timed Out, LLC presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on the first and second causes of action(Walters Depo., Vol. II, 9:13-10:14; 19:17-23; Walters Depo., Vol. I, 68:8-69:15; Davila Dec., pars. 2-6.) Defendant Heritage Management fails to maintain its initial summary adjudication burden as to the third cause of action. Paragraphs 76 -77 of the complaint allege that Heritage negligently hired, retained, and supervised its employees "WALTERS, ALLISON, and SOBEY" as well as other employees. (The court assumes that Sobey and Sovey are the same person). Defendants' separate statement fails to show that the employees were competent or that Heritage lacked actual or constructive notice regarding the employees' fitness to perform their work. In all events, even though plaintiff alleges that Heritage was negligent with regard to employee Allison, there is no mention of Mr. Allison in the motion or separate statement. A triable issue exists as to whether J. Dog had exclusive control over Defendants' social media and a triable issue also exists as to whether JDog's independent Contractor status renders Defendants' immune from liability. (as fully reported in reporter's notes) Order approved by parties is signed.(see scanned order for details)Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
